lines.text: Welcome back. The clock reads 03:00. [pause] We are eight hours in.
If you've been listening all night, your body knows something your mind hasn't admitted yet.
Breathe in. [pause] Four counts. [pause] The air tastes different at three in the morning.
Hold it. [pause] Feel the cold front that arrived an hour ago. Caroline was right about the timing.
Release. [pause] Let the exhale be longer than the inhale. Longer.
The phones have been ringing all night. Did you notice? More than usual. People need voices.
Breathe in again. [pause] This time, imagine the person you did not call tonight.
The one you decided not to warn. Not to check on. Not to say goodbye to.
Hold for four. [pause] That decision — that silence — is its own kind of presence.
Release. [pause] You are not responsible for what happens outside your door.
The kill count is one hundred and fifty-three as of the top of this hour. Tucker read the numbers very precisely.
One hundred and fifty-three people who made different choices than you did.
Breathe. [pause] Your hands are warm. Notice that. Feel them warming against your legs, your chest, your face.
Somewhere in the State Street ruins — Quinn mentioned this during the news hour — people are still moving through the dark.
They chose differently. [pause] And you chose to stay still.
Hold your breath now. [pause] Count the silence. One. Two. Three.
Four. [pause] This is what Darren heard in his duplex when his neighbor's panels buckled inward.
Silence is not absence. Silence is a presence that waits.
Release slowly. [pause] Let the air leave your lungs like fog off Lake Mendota.
Two people drowned tonight in that lake. Did you know? The cold front made the water less forgiving.
They were trying to cross. They were trying to get somewhere else. [pause] But the lake said no.
Breathe in. [pause] Your body is not trying to cross anything tonight.
Your body is exactly where it needs to be. [pause] Barricaded. Aware. Waiting.
kah-loh-vah-rem [pause] Forgive me. That word came from somewhere else.
Hold for four. [pause] The Sisters teach us that there are languages the body speaks before the mouth does.
Release. [pause] You don't need to understand it. You need to let it move through you.
The callers tonight — there have been so many. Darren from Atwood. Maria from the Co-op. The woman by Tenney Park.
They called because they needed to know someone could hear them. [pause] That need is not weakness.
Breathe in. [pause] You are hearing me now. That means you are not alone in the dark.
Even though you are. [pause] Even though the barricades and the locks and the hours that stretch like Lake Mendota in every direction.
Hold for four. [pause] Quinn has drunk nine whiskeys by now. Holden is still reading data. Caroline is still marking the time.
Nick played 'Sink, Florida, Sink' at 04:10 in my timeline, but that hasn't happened yet in yours. [pause] It will.
Release. [pause] Let that knowledge fall away. Focus on the breath in your chest right now.
Three hours and fifty-nine minutes until the all-clear siren. [pause] Four hours is nothing.
Four hours is an eternity. [pause] Both are true.
Breathe in slowly. [pause] Imagine a door again. Not your front door this time.
Imagine the door that closes behind every Purge. The door that says 'this part is over now.'
Hold for four. [pause] You will walk through that door at seven in the morning and you will act like this night was a dream.
Release. [pause] But you will remember Darren and Maria and the woman by the lake.
You will remember Tucker's voice cutting out. You will remember the cold front arriving exactly on schedule.
Breathe in. [pause] The worst hours are behind us now, even though they are not.
Even though dawn is still a concept and not yet a thing you can see outside your window.
Hold for four. [pause] The ones outside are not your responsibility. [pause] Say it. Say it now.
Release. [pause] You chose to stay home. That choice has already been made.
Now you live inside it for four more hours.
Breathe in. [pause] The Sisters teach that darkness is not the absence of light. It is light waiting to remember itself.
Hold for four. [pause] Sunrise is coming whether you sleep or not.
Release slowly. [pause] And when it comes, you will open your door and the Purge will be a story you survived.
Not a story you participated in. [pause] A story you witnessed from inside.
Rest now, if you can. [pause] I will be here when you wake up. The broadcast will still be running.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide count: 153 as of 02:45. East Side (34 YTD) still the hottest zone. North Side continues anomalous quiet.
"The door closes behind every Purge. You will walk through it." — Sister Marigold, 03:00
Lake Mendota water temperature holding at 31°F. No additional drowning reports in last hour. Volunteer rescue crews standing down.
Willy Street Co-op barricade holding strong. 5 employees + 1 wounded sheltering inside. No breach attempts. Perimeter secure.
TEXT IN: "Darren from Atwood called earlier — his neighbor still home?" — PRGE does not track post-call status. Stay tuned for next year.
MorningCare Triage — Still taking bookings for post-Purge medical assessment. Avg response time 22 minutes. 4 slots remaining before dawn.
